description: Machine-readable companion to the Epic AL portable-surface conformance gap inventory. v0.3.0 reflected Epic AS promoting the MCP Client Container translation contract as the ninth expected packet: tuvren.providers.mcp. v0.4.0 reflects Epic BC closing the Tooling block and promoting the capability-orchestration-integration conformance plan as a required authoritative source for tuvren.shared.core; the capability-orchestration surface (four execution classes, MCP-as-binding, exposure/invocation policy, per-class observation limits, conceptual invariant) is now fully tracked under tuvren.shared.core. v0.4.1 is a path-only update: GH issue #87 (Sovereign Polyglot Framework Restructure) M1.3 relocated the kernel authority packet and CDDL grammar from `boundaries/kernel/contracts/protocol/spec/` to root `spec/kernel/`; no portability scope, classification, or closure state changed. v0.4.2 is likewise path-only: 87-M5.1c moved the MCP authority packet cross-port to `spec/tools/mcp/authority-packet.json` and consolidated the tools-owned conformance plans (tool-contracts-extended, capability-policy, capability-orchestration-integration, both execution-class plans, providers-mcp-client) under `spec/conformance/tools/plans/`; no portability scope, classification, or closure state changed. v0.4.3 is likewise path-only: 87-M6.1b lifted the react-driver authority packet and the driver-api/react-driver runner-plan and typespec sources to `spec/runners/` (packet, typespec, bindings, artifacts) and `spec/conformance/runners/` (plans, scenarios); packet, plan, and check identities are unchanged. v0.5.0 reflects 87-M6.4b, the semantic-identity rename mandated by the epic: the react packet, plan, and check identities themselves change from driver-vocabulary to runner-vocabulary (`tuvren.framework.react-driver` becomes `tuvren.framework.react-runner`, `driver-api-*`/`react-driver-*` plan and check families become `runner-api-*`/`react-runner-*`), unlike the prior path-only moves; this is the one issue-mandated identity churn and no packet topology or closure state otherwise changed. v0.6.0 is likewise path-only: 87-M8.1a lifted the event-stream-sse authority packet and its typespec/fixture/plan sources to `spec/streaming/sse/` and `spec/conformance/streaming/`; packet, plan, and check identities are unchanged. v0.7.0 is likewise path-only: 87-M8.2a lifted the telemetry semconv authority packet from `boundaries/telemetry/semconv/spec/authority-packet.json` to `spec/telemetry/authority-packet.json` and its authored semconv YAML source from `telemetry/semconv/` to `spec/telemetry/semconv/`; packet, plan, and check identities are unchanged. v0.8.0 is likewise path-only: 87-M8.3 moved the rust-kernel interop suite (conformance evidence, distinct from the `spec/interop/` kernel IPC transport contract) from `boundaries/framework/interop/rust-kernel/` to `spec/conformance/interop/rust-kernel/`, updating the `tuvren.framework.interop-rust-kernel` packetPath and its 2 requiredAuthoritativeSources sourcePath entries; packet, plan, and check identities are unchanged. The portability gate at `tools/scripts/portability-gate.ts` consumes this file to enforce that the on-disk packet topology, standing exceptions, and required authoritative sources have not drifted from the inventory's recorded closure state. Editing either the human inventory MD or this JSON without the matching counterpart change must be treated as drift and rejected during review. The gate fails closed if this file is missing, malformed, or names paths that do not exist on disk. v0.9.0 reflects ADR-060 (issue #99) adding the tenth expected packet: tuvren.framework.host-session at spec/host/session/authority-packet.json, the experimental duplex session frame vocabulary sub-surface under the host port (wrapped canonical events, client_invocation dispatch, session_rejection, and the inbound client_result/approval_response/steer/cancel family), certified by the host-session conformance plan under spec/conformance/engine/plans/. The surface is classified portable by intent (the inbound vocabulary is canonical cross-language truth like the outbound stream) while TypeScript remains the only advertising implementation; capability selection keeps other ports non-applicable until they attach a session binding. v0.10.0 reflects M8 (issues #102/#104): ADR-063 added @tuvren/remote-session (the transport-agnostic reattachable session-lifecycle seam under typescript/host/remote-session) and @tuvren/session-client (the zero-dependency reference remote peer under typescript/host/session-client); neither owns a new authority packet — the session-lifecycle semantics they realize are certified as an extension of the existing tuvren.framework.host-session packet's conformance plan (host-session.json 0.2.0 -> 0.3.0: reattach redelivery, grace-window expiry, dispatch timeout, sequence continuity across two sinks), and the M6 network-evidence claim (reconnect-redelivery dedup, kill-durability) is certified as an extension of the tuvren-client-execution-class plan (0.2.0 -> 0.3.0) driven through the real binding -> session -> transport composition. ADR-064 added the sanitizeToolResult host seam, certified by a new sanitize-seam check set on runtime-api-callables-extended.json (0.7.0 -> 0.8.0) following ADR-044 §4's precedent that secret absence is asserted by conformance against persisted and captured surfaces, never by trusting the hook was called. Both packages are TypeScript-only host implementations today (no packet, no portability gap opened): the real cross-process, real-socket claim these milestones proved remains package-test-only evidence under typescript/host/repl/test/repl-serve-ws.e2e.test.ts.

requiredAuthoritativeSources.rationale: KRT-AL002 G2: kernel CDDL grammar must remain registered authority

KRT-AL002 G1 (absorbed): consolidated core TypeSpec at spec/core/typespec/main.tsp (relocated from boundaries/shared/contracts/core/spec/ by GH issue #87 M2.2) is the canonical authority source for tuvren.shared.core (Epic AP)
KRT-AL002 G3: SSE projection TypeSpec source
KRT-AL002 G3: WHATWG-normative SSE byte-trace fixtures
KRT-AL002 G4: kernel gRPC services proto
KRT-AS008: MCP Client Container translation, validation, auth, and transport parity conformance plan
KRT-AL002 G4: kernel gRPC types proto
KRT-AL002 G5: rust-kernel interop suite manifest
KRT-AL002 G5: rust-kernel interop suite manifest JSON Schema (authoritative source for the manifest shape)

KRT-BC001: cross-class capability-orchestration integration conformance plan; required authority for the capability-orchestration surface under tuvren.shared.core (four execution classes, MCP-as-binding, exposure/invocation policy, per-class observation limits, conceptual invariant)

ADR-063 (issue #102, M8): the host-session plan's 0.3.0 bump adds the session-lifecycle check set (reattach redelivery, grace-window expiry, dispatch timeout, sequence continuity across two sinks) realized by @tuvren/remote-session; the plan bump is required authority for that surface, unchanged in packet topology.

ADR-064 §4 (issue #104, M8), following ADR-044 §4's precedent: the runtime-api-callables-extended plan's 0.8.0 bump adds the sanitize-seam check set asserting AgentConfig.sanitizeToolResult's scrubbed form (and marker absence) against both the persisted kernel tool_result record and the captured canonical tool.result event.

M6 (issue #102): the tuvren-client-execution-class plan's 0.3.0 bump adds the network-evidence check set (reconnect-redelivery dedup, kill-durability) realized in-process through the real binding -> session -> transport composition; the real cross-process, real-socket claim stays package-test-only evidence under typescript/host/repl/test/repl-serve-ws.e2e.test.ts.
